canonical_definition: A dataset of real-world viral claims spread online, verified by journalists, used to assess models' ability to handle real-world misinformation.
papers: Towards Understanding Factual Knowledge of Large Language Models

definitions.text: A dataset of real-world viral claims spread online, verified by journalists, used to assess models' ability to handle real-world misinformation.
definitions.paper: Towards Understanding Factual Knowledge of Large Language Models

definitions.text: A real-world human-written misinformation dataset focused on political fake news, used as a source for generating new misinformation and as a baseline for comparison.

characterization: A dataset of real-world viral claims spread online, verified by journalists, used to assess models' ability to handle real-world misinformation.

iclr24: Towards Understanding Factual Knowledge of Large Language Models
